---
title: "Breakout Trading Strategy — Backtest & Analysis"
---

```{=html}
<div id="stats-banner" style="display:flex; justify-content:center; gap:2rem; flex-wrap:wrap; margin:1.5rem 0 2rem;">
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-number" data-target="30">0</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Total Trades</div>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-number" data-target="43" data-suffix="%">0%</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Win Rate</div>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-number" data-target="-3.07" data-decimal="true">0</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Sharpe Ratio</div>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-number" data-target="-1.11" data-decimal="true" data-suffix="%">0%</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Max Drawdown</div>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-number" data-target="1.32" data-decimal="true">0</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Profit Factor</div>
  </div>
</div>

<style>
.stat-card {
  background: rgba(15,23,42,0.9);
  border: 1px solid rgba(0,212,255,0.3);
  border-radius: 16px;
  padding: 1.2rem 2rem;
  text-align: center;
  min-width: 140px;
  transition: all 0.4s ease;
  cursor: default;
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.stat-card::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  top: -50%; left: -50%;
  width: 200%; height: 200%;
  background: conic-gradient(transparent, rgba(0,212,255,0.1), transparent 30%);
  animation: rotateBorder 4s linear infinite;
}
@keyframes rotateBorder {
  100% { transform: rotate(360deg); }
}
.stat-card:hover {
  border-color: #00d4ff;
  box-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(0,212,255,0.3), 0 0 60px rgba(0,212,255,0.1);
  transform: translateY(-5px) scale(1.05);
}
.stat-number {
  font-family: 'Orbitron', monospace;
  font-size: 2rem;
  font-weight: 900;
  color: #00d4ff;
  position: relative;
  z-index: 1;
}
.stat-label {
  font-family: 'Rajdhani', sans-serif;
  color: #94a3b8;
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  margin-top: 0.3rem;
  position: relative;
  z-index: 1;
}
</style>

<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
    entries.forEach(entry => {
      if (entry.isIntersecting) {
        animateCounters();
        observer.disconnect();
      }
    });
  });
  const banner = document.getElementById('stats-banner');
  if (banner) observer.observe(banner);

  function animateCounters() {
    document.querySelectorAll('.stat-number').forEach(el => {
      const target = parseFloat(el.dataset.target);
      const isDecimal = el.dataset.decimal === 'true';
      const suffix = el.dataset.suffix || '';
      const duration = 2000;
      const start = performance.now();
      function update(now) {
        const elapsed = now - start;
        const progress = Math.min(elapsed / duration, 1);
        const eased = 1 - Math.pow(1 - progress, 3);
        const current = target * eased;
        el.textContent = (isDecimal ? current.toFixed(2) : Math.round(current)) + suffix;
        if (progress < 1) requestAnimationFrame(update);
      }
      requestAnimationFrame(update);
    });
  }

  // Smooth reveal on scroll
  const sections = document.querySelectorAll('.level2');
  const sectionObserver = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
    entries.forEach(entry => {
      if (entry.isIntersecting) {
        entry.target.style.opacity = '1';
        entry.target.style.transform = 'translateY(0)';
      }
    });
  }, { threshold: 0.1 });
  sections.forEach(s => sectionObserver.observe(s));
});
</script>
```

## Strategy Logic

This strategy implements a **Donchian Channel breakout system** on USO (United States Oil Fund ETF). Each trading day, we check whether the closing price breaks above the 20-day high (long breakout) or below the 20-day low (short breakout). When a breakout is detected, we enter a position of 100 shares in the breakout direction, set a profit target at 2× the 14-day ATR from the entry price, a stop-loss at 1× ATR, and a 10-trading-day timeout — if neither the target nor the stop is hit within 10 days, we exit at market close. The strategy is backtested out-of-sample on data from June 2023 to April 2025, with the prior period (Jan 2022–June 2023) reserved for parameter calibration, ensuring no data snooping.

## Asset Selection

**USO (United States Oil Fund ETF)** was selected because crude oil is one of the most actively traded commodities with strong momentum and mean-reversion regimes driven by macro catalysts (OPEC decisions, inventory reports, geopolitical events). A scan of multiple ETFs confirmed that USO exhibited frequent, clean breakouts from consolidation ranges over the past three years, making it well-suited for a channel-based breakout strategy.

## Breakout Definition

- **Indicator:** Donchian Channel with a 20-day lookback period
- **Long breakout:** Close > highest high of the prior 20 trading days
- **Short breakout:** Close < lowest low of the prior 20 trading days
- **Profit target:** Entry price ± 2.0 × ATR(14)
- **Stop-loss:** Entry price ∓ 1.0 × ATR(14)
- **Timeout:** 10 trading days — exit at market close if neither target nor stop is triggered
- **Position size:** 100 shares per trade
- **No commissions or slippage modeled** (assumption: liquid ETF with tight spreads)

## Performance Summary

```{=html}
<iframe src="summary_table.html" width="100%" height="400" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Equity Curve

```{=html}
<iframe src="equity_curve.html" width="100%" height="500" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Drawdown

```{=html}
<iframe src="drawdown.html" width="100%" height="400" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Breakout Signals & Trade Entries/Exits

```{=html}
<iframe src="breakout_signals.html" width="100%" height="750" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Trade Return Distribution

```{=html}
<iframe src="trade_distribution.html" width="100%" height="450" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Trade Outcome Breakdown

```{=html}
<iframe src="trade_outcomes.html" width="100%" height="400" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Trade Blotter

Download: [trades.csv](trades.csv)

```{=html}
<iframe src="blotter_table.html" width="100%" height="600" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Discussion

**Strengths:**

- The strategy is fully systematic and transparent — all parameters are explicitly defined.
- Walk-forward design (train on 2022–mid 2023, test on mid 2023–2025) prevents data snooping.
- The 2:1 reward-to-risk ratio (profit target = 2× ATR, stop = 1× ATR) is a sound risk management principle.

**Weaknesses:**

- Win rate of ~43% is below 50%, which is typical for breakout strategies but requires large winners to compensate.
- The annualized Sharpe ratio is negative (-3.07) because the strategy's modest 0.47% annual return is well below the 3.75% risk-free rate. In a lower-rate environment, results would look better.
- 16 out of 30 trades hit stop-loss, indicating many false breakouts — a known challenge with Donchian channels in range-bound markets.

**Risks:**

- Crude oil can enter extended consolidation phases where breakouts repeatedly fail.
- No transaction cost modeling — real performance would be slightly worse.
- Static 20-day channel and 14-day ATR parameters may not be optimal across all market regimes.

**Improvements:**

- Add a **volume confirmation filter** (only enter if breakout bar volume > 1.5× average) to reduce false breakouts.
- Implement **adaptive channel width** based on recent volatility regime.
- Use a **trailing stop** instead of a fixed stop to capture larger trends.
- Expand to **multiple assets** and trade the portfolio to diversify signal risk.
- Test **longer Donchian periods** (e.g., 55-day) which may produce fewer but higher-quality breakouts.
